title: श्री शिव नामावली अष्टकम्
devanagariText:
शिवो महेश्वरः शम्भुः पिनाकी शशिशेखरः।
वामदेवो विरूपाक्षः कपर्दी नीललोहितः॥१॥

शंकरः शूलपाणिश्च खट्वाङ्गी विष्णुवल्लभः।
शिपिविष्टोऽम्बिकानाथः श्रीकण्ठो भक्तवत्सलः॥२॥

भवः शर्वस्त्रिलोकेशः शितिकण्ठः शिवाप्रियः।
उग्रः कपाली कामारी अन्धकासुरसूदनः॥३॥

गङ्गाधरो ललाटाक्षः कालकालः कृपानिधिः।
भीमः परशुहस्तश्च मृगपाणिर्जटाधरः॥४॥

कैलासवासी कवची कठोरस्त्रिपुरान्तकः।
वृषाङ्को वृषभारूढो भस्मोद्धूलितविग्रहः॥५॥

सामप्रियो स्वरमयः त्रयीमूर्तिरनीश्वरः।
सर्वज्ञः परमात्मा च सोमसूर्याग्निलोचनः॥६॥

हविर्यज्ञमयो योगी सोमकासुरमर्दनः।
महाकालः सदाशिवो विश्वेश्वरो महाबलः॥७॥

महादेवः प्रजापालो रुद्रः सर्वसुरेश्वरः।
मृत्युञ्जयो निर्गुणश्च आदियोगीश्वरः परः॥८॥

नामाष्टकमिदं पुण्यं शिवस्य परमात्मनः।
यः पठेत् प्रयतो नित्यं शिवलोकमवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री शिव नामावली अष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
Shivo Maheshvarah Shambhuh Pinaki Shashishekharah.
Vamadevo Virupakshah Kapardi Nilalohitah.||1||

Shankarah Shulapanishcha Khatvangi Vishnuvallabhah.
Shipivishtombikanathah Shrikantho Bhaktavatsalah.||2||

Bhavah Sharvas Trilokesah Shitikanthah Shivapriyah.
Ugrah Kapali Kamari Andhakasurasudanah.||3||

Gangadharo Lalatakshah Kalakalah Kripanidhih.
Bhimah Parashuhastashcha Mrigapanir Jataadharah.||4||

Kailasavasi Kavachi Kathorastripurantakah.
Vrishanko Vrishabharudho Bhasmoddhulit-Vigrahah.||5||

Samapriyo Svaramayah Trayimurtir Anishvarah.
Sarvajnah Paramatma Cha Somasuryagniloch-anah.||6||

Haviryajnamayo Yogi Somakasura-Mardanah.
Mahakalah Sadashivo Vishveshvaro Mahabalah.||7||

Mahadevah Prajapaalo Rudrah Sarvasureshvarah.
Mrityunjayo Nirgunashcha Adiyogishvarah Parah.||8||

Namashtakam Idam Punyam Shivasya Paramatmanah.
Yah Pathet Prayato Nityam Shivalokam Avapnuyat.

|| Iti Shri Shiva Namavali Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
शिव, महेश्वर, शम्भु, पिनाकधारी, शशिशेखर, वामदेव, विरूपाक्ष, कपर्दी (जटाधारी) और नीललोहित।

शंकर, शूलपाणि, खट्वाङ्गधारी, विष्णुवल्लभ, शिपिविष्ट, अम्बिकानाथ, श्रीकण्ठ और भक्तवत्सल।

भव, शर्व, त्रिलोकेश, शितिकण्ठ (नीलकण्ठ), शिवप्रिय, उग्र, कपाली (खप्परधारी), कामारि (कामदेव शत्रु) और अन्धकासुर का संहारक।

गंगाधर, ललाटाक्ष (ललाट में नेत्र), काल के भी काल, कृपानिधि, भीम, परशुहस्त, मृगपाणि (मृग धारक) और जटाधर।

कैलासवासी, कवचधारी, कठोर, त्रिपुरान्तक, वृषाङ्क (वृषभ चिह्न वाले), वृषभारूढ (नन्दी पर सवार) और भस्म से विभूषित शरीर।

सामवेदप्रिय, स्वरमय, त्रयीमूर्ति (तीन वेदों के स्वरूप), अनीश्वर (जिनके ऊपर कोई ईश्वर नहीं), सर्वज्ञ, परमात्मा, सोम-सूर्य-अग्नि नेत्र वाले।

हवि-यज्ञमय, योगी, सोमकासुर मर्दन करने वाले, महाकाल, सदाशिव, विश्वेश्वर और महाबल।

महादेव, प्रजापालक, रुद्र, सर्वसुरेश्वर, मृत्युञ्जय, निर्गुण, आदियोगीश्वर और परब्रह्म।

शिव परमात्मा का यह पुण्यदायी नामाष्टक जो पवित्र भाव से नित्य पठन करता है, वह शिवलोक प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Reciting Shiva's sacred names destroys all sins
Each verse contains multiple powerful names of Lord Shiva
Invokes the combined blessings of all forms of shiva
Easy to memorize and recite daily
Ideal for Rudra Abhisheka and Shiva Puja
Leads to attainment of Shivaloka after death
description: The Shiva Namavali Ashtakam, known in Sanskrit as श्री शिव नामावली अष्टकम्, is an eight-verse devotional hymn in praise of Lord Shiva. This sacred composition is sourced from Shiva Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to easy to memorize and recite daily and grant the combined blessings of all forms of Shiva. It is also said to lead to attainment of Shivaloka after death. It is especially recommended on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Pradosh for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Shiva Namavali Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shiva Namavali Ashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight verses of Lord Shiva's powerful names for daily worship, devotion, and liberation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Shiva Namavali Ashtakam and how is it structured?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiva Namavali Ashtakam is an eight-verse hymn (ashtaka means a set of eight) consisting almost entirely of the divine names (namavali) of Lord Shiva. Each verse packs six to eight names of Shiva drawn from his Puranic, Tantric and Vedic appellations: Shiva, Maheshvara, Shambhu, Pinaki, Shashishekhara, Vamadeva, Virupaksha, Kapardi, Nilalohita, Shulapani, Khatvangi, Mahadeva, Mrityunjaya, Sadashiva and many more. The compact namavali format makes it both a stotra and a memory-aid for the principal names of Shiva used in archana and abhisheka rituals.

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Shiva Namavali Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn is preserved in the Shiva Purana namavali corpus, alongside related compositions such as the Shiva Ashtottara Shatanamavali (108 names) and the Shiva Sahasranama (1000 names). The eight-verse form is structured for daily householder recitation when time does not permit the longer 108 or 1008 name recitations. It is freely available in standard Shaiva stotra anthologies and is taught in Smarta and Shaiva-Siddhanta paths as part of the basic Shiva-archana training.

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Shiva Namavali Ashtakam and Shiva Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Namavali Ashtakam is a compact eight-verse hymn containing roughly 50 to 60 of Shiva's most important names, suitable for a 5-minute daily recitation. The Shiva Sahasranama (preserved in the Linga Purana, Shiva Purana and Mahabharata Anushasana Parva) is a thousand-name hymn requiring 25 to 30 minutes. Both serve archana, but the Sahasranama is typically used for special occasions like Pradosh, Maha Shivaratri and Shravan Mondays with bilva-archana, while the Namavali Ashtakam is used as a quick daily Shiva-smarana before puja, while bathing, or on the way to a temple.

seoFaqs.question: Which names of Shiva are most powerful within this hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: Each of the names carries distinct sadhana significance. Mrityunjaya in verse 8 is the death-conquering name and resonates with the Mahamrityunjaya Mantra. Tripurantaka in verse 5 is invoked for victory over enemies and inner asuras. Mahakal and Sadashiva in verse 7 are invoked for liberation from time. Gangadhara in verse 4 is invoked for purification, since Shiva bears the river of liberation in his locks. Bhaktavatsala in verse 2 is invoked for personal grace toward the devotee. Devotees often select a particular name from the Ashtakam as a daily japa focus depending on their need.

seoFaqs.question: How should the Shiva Namavali Ashtakam be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional recommendation is daily recitation after morning bath, facing east or north, before a Shiva image or lingam, with offering of bilva leaves, white flowers and water abhisheka. On Mondays, Pradosh days (the trayodashi twilight), Maha Shivaratri night and the entire month of Shravan, recitation is multiplied to 8, 16 or 108 times. The phalashruti states that whoever recites it daily with purity attains Shivaloka after death. Many householders use it as the namavali component of their daily Rudra Abhisheka, recited as water is poured over the lingam.

seoFaqs.question: Can children and beginners recite the Shiva Namavali Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes, the hymn is one of the most beginner-friendly Shaiva stotras precisely because it consists of names rather than complex grammatical sentences. Each name is short and rhythmically structured, making memorisation straightforward. Children in many South Indian and Maharashtrian families learn this as their first Shiva-stotra after the Mahamrityunjaya Mantra. There are no caste, gender, age or initiation restrictions; the hymn is part of the open Puranic Shaiva liturgy. Reciting the names alone is held to confer all the merit of formal Shiva-archana when time or means do not permit a full puja.
